title: Yowie in Acacia Hills yowie sightings and damage speculation
publication_or_organisation: The Northern Territory News, 17 October 2014; News Corp Australia / Library & Archives NT
australian_relation: Territory Stories OCR from an NT newspaper item explicitly naming Acacia Hills and yowie sightings or damage speculation.
coordinate_evidence_note: Territory Stories OCR names yowie sightings in the Acacia Hills region; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Acacia Hills town result.
evidence_summary: Yowie! Let's hope the bark worse than bite. It's hard to imagine what could cause this sort of damage. A Yowie expert, who preferred to remain anonymous, has examined the photos and recognised the distinctive marking on the trees. "It could be a Yowie, there's certainly some strange things that live" in a way very similar to what has happened here. "Yowies are not really know for climbing but they do have a powerful leap." Yowie sightings have been long documented in the Acacia Hills region.
